content.problemStatement:
Suzumo has a chessboard with NNN rows and MMM columns. In one step, he can choose two cells of the chessboard which share a common edge (that has not been cut yet) and cut this edge.

Formally, the chessboard is split into two or more pieces if it is possible to partition its cells into two non-empty subsets S1S_1S1​ and S2S_2S2​ (S1∩S2=∅S_1\cap S_2 = \emptysetS1​∩S2​=∅, ∣S1∣+∣S2∣=NM|S_1|+|S_2|=NM∣S1​∣+∣S2​∣=NM) such that there is no pair of cells c1,c2c_1, c_2c1​,c2​ (c1∈S1,c2∈S2c_1 \in S_1, c_2 \in S_2c1​∈S1​,c2​∈S2​) which share a common edge that has not been cut.

Suzumo does not want the board to split into two or more pieces. Compute the maximum number of steps he can perform while satisfying this condition.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and M.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum possible number of steps.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the board has 2 rows and 4 columns. The maximum number of steps that can be performed is 2 * 4 - 2 - 4 + 1 = 3. For the second test case, the board has 3 rows and 3 columns. The maximum number of steps that can be performed is 3 * 3 - 3 - 3 + 1 = 4. For the third test case, the board has 6 rows and 8 columns. The maximum number of steps that can be performed is 6 * 8 - 6 - 8 + 1 = 35. For the fourth test case, the board has 7 rows and 5 columns. The maximum number of steps that can be performed is 7 * 5 - 7 - 5 + 1 = 24.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by calculating the maximum number of steps that can be performed without splitting the board into two or more pieces. This can be achieved by finding the minimum number of edges that need to be left uncut to keep the board connected. For an N x M board, the minimum number of edges that need to be left uncut is N + M - 2. Therefore, the maximum number of steps that can be performed is (N * M - 1) - (N + M - 2) = N * M - N - M + 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) — the time complexity is constant because the calculation of the maximum number of steps only involves a few arithmetic operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant because only a few variables are used to store the input and the result.

code.Python3:
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, M = map(int, input().split())
    print(N * M - N - M + 1)
